mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in quarterly GDP growth signals expanding economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 5.25% signals aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises above 5.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI inflation and rising Fed rates create a 'Goldilocks' environment where growth is stable and rates are not overly restrictive, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL < 3% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tight labor markets, which pressures wage growth and corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production above 112.5 indicates robust manufacturing activity, which supports equity earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 112.5.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month surge in housing starts above 1,500 thousand units signals strong consumer confidence and demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1500.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates strong consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue visibility.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 30% of periods where RSAFS and HOUST both rise by > 1% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in payroll employment below 137,684 thousand signals weakening labor demand, which reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS falls below 137684.
